records.ety: [F. dmoraliser; pref. d- (L. dis- or de) + moraliser. See Moralize.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Demoralized (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Demoralizing.]

defs: To corrupt or undermine in morals; to destroy or lessen the effect of moral principles on; to render corrupt or untrustworthy in morals, in discipline, in courage, spirit, etc.; to weaken in spirit or efficiency.
quotes: The demoralizing example of profligate power and prosperous crime.
The vices of the nobility had demoralized the army.
